item.title: Industrious Coworking Day: Summer Series
item.description:
Tuesday, June 30, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Industrious, 4201 Main St, Houston, TX, 77002

Register here: https://luma.com/lpkh8qjc ​Spend the day working from Industrious at the ion. ​Join us for a free coworking day and experience what a better workday can feel like. Bring your laptop, settle in, enjoy coffee and refreshments, and get your work done in a space designed for focus, comfort, and connection. ​Whether you’re working solo, meeting a colleague, or looking for a new home base for your team, this is an easy way to experience Industrious firsthand. ​During the day, you’ll have access to: ​• Thoughtfully designed shared workspace • Coffee, breakfast, and snacks • Comfortable common areas • On-site support from our local team • Opportunities to connect with other professionals in the space ​As well as our Grant Writing Workshop! ​Industrious offers coworking, private offices, suites, meeting rooms, and flexible workplace options for individuals and teams. With 250+ locations across 80+ cities globally, we make it easier to find a workspace that works for you. Space is limited. RSVP to reserve your spot. ​Parking: Parking is free for the first 3 hours, then $10 for the remainder of the day. ​We look forward to welcoming you!

From Ion District

item.title: Latino Founders Forum - Lymbar Hosted
item.description:
Tuesday, June 30, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: The Lymbar

Register here: https://luma.com/Latino-Founders-Forum The June edition of the Latino Founders Forum Houston is on June 30th ⚽🎉 ​After a strong launch that brought out 50+ founders, operators, investors, creatives, and professionals, the forum is keeping the momentum going with a special World Cup Celebration edition of our monthly gathering. ​Yes, it’s called Latino Founders Forum — but it’s not only for Latinos. Anyone is welcome to pull up, grab a drink, wear their favorite soccer jersey, make meaningful connections, and be part of the community. ​This is a monthly event for people building real things in Houston. While the forum is rooted in supporting and elevating Latino founders, the room is open to anyone who values entrepreneurship, culture, community, and genuine relationships. They’re back at The Lymbar inside the Ion for another high-quality happy hour designed to bring together founders, funders, advisors, builders, creatives, and professionals who want to meet ambitious people in a room that actually feels worth being in. ​This month, they’re bringing a World Cup vibe to the night and inviting guests to wear their favorite soccer jersey as we celebrate culture, connection, and community in Houston. Expect a great atmosphere, strong energy, good drinks, and the kind of conversations that make these events worth showing up for. ​Come for the jerseys, drinks, and festive energy. Stay for the people, the conversations, and the chance to be part of Houston’s growing startup and business community. ​If you came to one of the earlier events, you already know the energy. If you haven’t yet, this is a great one to start with. ​Built by founders. Powered by community. Open to everyone.

From Ion District

item.title: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas (Late)
item.description:
Tuesday, June 30, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino, 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV, 89103, United States

This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Official Tickets Resale Tickets Comedy Cellar Las Vegas brings one of the most legendary names in stand-up comedy to the heart of Las Vegas. Located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino, this intimate comedy club is the West Coast home of New York City’s world-famous Comedy Cellar, the venue that helped launch the careers of comedy icons including Dave Chappelle, Chris Rock, Jon Stewart, Ray Romano, Amy Schumer, Aziz Ansari, and countless others. Unlike traditional comedy shows featuring a single headliner, Comedy Cellar presents a carefully curated lineup of professional comedians every night. Audiences can expect multiple performers during each show, featuring a mix of nationally touring headliners, television favorites, Comedy Central veterans, Netflix performers, and rising stars from around the world. No two shows are exactly alike, making every visit a unique experience. Surprise drop-in appearances from major comedians performing elsewhere in Las Vegas have become part of the club’s reputation, giving audiences the chance to see comedy superstars in an intimate setting. Event Details Venue: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino Location: 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89103 Show Schedule: Nightly performances throughout the year Age Requirement: Recommended for audiences 18+ due to mature comedy content About the Venue Opened in 2018, Comedy Cellar Las Vegas was designed to recreate the atmosphere and energy of the original Greenwich Village club that has become one of the most respected comedy venues in the world. The 300-seat showroom offers excellent sightlines and an intimate setting that allows audiences to experience stand-up comedy exactly as it was meant to be seen: up close and personal. The venue combines the excitement of a Las Vegas night out with the authentic feel of a classic New York comedy club. Guests can enjoy a comfortable showroom environment while watching some of the best comedians working today perform in a setting that encourages spontaneity, crowd interaction, and unforgettable moments. Conveniently located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino just minutes from the Las Vegas Strip, the venue offers easy access for both tourists and local comedy fans. What to Expect Expect a fast-paced evening featuring multiple comedians performing throughout the show. The Comedy Cellar is known for showcasing a wide variety of comedic styles, from observational humor and storytelling to sharp social commentary, crowd work, and cutting-edge stand-up. Whether you’re discovering a future comedy star or watching a seasoned veteran perfect their craft, every show delivers the authentic comedy club experience that has made the Comedy Cellar one of the most respected brands in stand-up comedy. For comedy fans visiting Las Vegas, Comedy Cellar is widely considered one of the best places in the city to experience live stand-up comedy any night of the week.

From Comedy Calendar

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Overcoming barriers to point-of-care nucleic acid amplification testing
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: BioScience Research Collaborative

In this thesis I address aspects of three key bottlenecks limiting clinical translation of isothermal nucleic acid amplification tests (isothermal NAATs) – (1) sample preparation, (2) assay integration and clinical validation, and (3) implementation analysis in real-world use environments. Addressing these bottlenecks is important to increase clinical access to highly sensitive and specific nucleic acid diagnostics where laboratory testing is unavailable. Currently, most people globally cannot access NAAT tests when they are needed. Isothermal NAATs, which operate at a single temperature reducing instrumentation requirements and processing time, have been developed to address this need. Despite substantial research and development on isothermal NAATs for many indications, very few have achieved widespread clinical adoption. To address the need for simple sample preparation suitable to the point-of-care, I focused on extraction-free sample preparation and developed a framework to guide the design and validation of extraction-free sample preparation methods based on gaps identified in the literature (Chapter 2 and 3). This framework focuses on identifying potential amplification inhibitors, determining the lytic efficiency of different methods, and creating appropriate model samples. This framework can be used by test developers to co-design an amplification assay and suitable extraction-free sample preparation workflow. When I then applied this framework in Chapter 3 to design an extraction-free sample preparation workflow for an assay detecting β-globin from buccal swabs and blood, I was able to design extraction-free sample preparation that consistently detected target from both buccal swabs and blood. I then applied the framework I developed in Chapters 4 and 5 to optimize extraction-free sample preparation for assays for two indications. In Chapter 4, to address the need for assay integration and clinical validation, I optimized SCD-RPA, an isothermal NAAT to diagnose sickle cell anemia and sickle cell trait. I integrated the assay with a modified version of the previously developed sample preparation workflow and validated it in a multi-country study with 161 patient samples collected in both a high- and a low-resource setting. SCD-RPA demonstrated robust performance with an overall genotype prediction accuracy of 96.9%. In Chapter 5, I applied the framework for extraction-free sample preparation to HPV-LAMP, a test being developed for cervical cancer screening, and achieved good analytical performance (30.4 HPV16 positive cells, 7.6 HPV18 positive cells, andhttps://riceuniversity.zoom.us/j/94882434656?pwd=JSDpS9UFAroXz0yoYgdKOezATjx20r.1 (https://riceuniversity.zoom.us/j/94882434656?pwd=JSDpS9UFAroXz0yoYgdKOezATjx20r.1)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

item.title: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas Showcase (Early)
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino, 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV, 89103, United States

This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Official Tickets Resale Tickets Comedy Cellar Las Vegas brings one of the most legendary names in stand-up comedy to the heart of Las Vegas. Located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino, this intimate comedy club is the West Coast home of New York City’s world-famous Comedy Cellar, the venue that helped launch the careers of comedy icons including Dave Chappelle, Chris Rock, Jon Stewart, Ray Romano, Amy Schumer, Aziz Ansari, and countless others. Unlike traditional comedy shows featuring a single headliner, Comedy Cellar presents a carefully curated lineup of professional comedians every night. Audiences can expect multiple performers during each show, featuring a mix of nationally touring headliners, television favorites, Comedy Central veterans, Netflix performers, and rising stars from around the world. No two shows are exactly alike, making every visit a unique experience. Surprise drop-in appearances from major comedians performing elsewhere in Las Vegas have become part of the club’s reputation, giving audiences the chance to see comedy superstars in an intimate setting. Event Details Venue: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino Location: 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89103 Show Schedule: Nightly performances throughout the year Age Requirement: Recommended for audiences 18+ due to mature comedy content About the Venue Opened in 2018, Comedy Cellar Las Vegas was designed to recreate the atmosphere and energy of the original Greenwich Village club that has become one of the most respected comedy venues in the world. The 300-seat showroom offers excellent sightlines and an intimate setting that allows audiences to experience stand-up comedy exactly as it was meant to be seen: up close and personal. The venue combines the excitement of a Las Vegas night out with the authentic feel of a classic New York comedy club. Guests can enjoy a comfortable showroom environment while watching some of the best comedians working today perform in a setting that encourages spontaneity, crowd interaction, and unforgettable moments. Conveniently located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino just minutes from the Las Vegas Strip, the venue offers easy access for both tourists and local comedy fans. What to Expect Expect a fast-paced evening featuring multiple comedians performing throughout the show. The Comedy Cellar is known for showcasing a wide variety of comedic styles, from observational humor and storytelling to sharp social commentary, crowd work, and cutting-edge stand-up. Whether you’re discovering a future comedy star or watching a seasoned veteran perfect their craft, every show delivers the authentic comedy club experience that has made the Comedy Cellar one of the most respected brands in stand-up comedy. For comedy fans visiting Las Vegas, Comedy Cellar is widely considered one of the best places in the city to experience live stand-up comedy any night of the week.

From Comedy Calendar

item.title: Mario Adrion LIVE at San Jose Improv
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: San Jose Improv, 62 S Second St, San Jose, California, 95113, United States

Official TicketsResale Tickets About the Performer Mario Adrion is a stand-up comedian from a small town in Germany who has taken the internet — and the world — by storm. With over 3.5 million followers across social media platforms, Mario built his fanbase through refreshingly honest and self-deprecating comedy that resonates with audiences everywhere. His comedy is uniquely vulnerable, playing with the struggles and stereotypes of being a retired male model and a European navigating life in the United States. Think fish-out-of-water humor with a sharp, charming twist — and a German accent that makes everything funnier. Over the past few years, Mario has toured his wildly popular “The German EFiciency Tour” around the world, selling out shows across the US, UK, Europe, and the Middle East. He filmed his debut hour-long special in January 2026 and is now embarking on an even bigger international tour, “The Superior Comedy Tour,” performing in clubs and theaters across the globe. Based out of Los Angeles, Mario Adrion is one of the hottest rising comics in the game right now. Event Details Date: Wednesday, July 1, 2026 Show Time: 7:30 PM PDT (Doors open at 6:00 PM) Venue: San Jose Improv, 62 S Second St, San Jose, CA 95113 Age Restriction: 21+ Event — Valid ID required at the door Content: Stand-up comedy — clean, relatable, and hilarious About the Venue The San Jose Improv is one of the Bay Area’s premier stand-up comedy clubs, located in the heart of downtown San Jose at 62 S Second St. Part of the nationally renowned Improv comedy club chain, the San Jose Improv has hosted some of the biggest names in comedy and continues to be the go-to destination for world-class stand-up in Silicon Valley. The intimate club setting creates an electric atmosphere where every seat feels close to the stage. The venue features a full-service bar and dining menu, with a mandatory two-item minimum purchase per guest in the showroom. Seating is general admission and first-come, first-served (with the exception of groups and premium booths), so arriving early is recommended to secure the best spots. The San Jose Improv is easily accessible in downtown San Jose, with multiple parking garages and public transit options nearby. The venue’s cozy, club-style layout ensures an up-close and personal comedy experience that larger theaters simply can’t match. What to Expect Expect a night of sharp, self-aware comedy that blends European sensibility with American pop culture observations. Mario Adrion’s show is a solo stand-up performance — no openers, just Mario and his signature storytelling style that has captivated millions online. His material draws heavily from his own life: the absurdity of being a former model, the culture shock of moving from Germany to the US, and the universal awkwardness of modern life. Fans of his social media content will recognize his style immediately — but live, it hits on a completely different level. This is a 21+ event, so bring your ID. The two-item minimum at the Improv means you’ll be sipping something while you laugh — which honestly just makes the whole experience better. No phones, no photography, no recording during the show. Just pure, unfiltered comedy. Ticket Information Price Range: $25 – $90 (plus fees) GA Ticket: $25 (+ fees) VIP Ticket: $40 (+ fees) — preferred seating Official TicketsResale Tickets This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.

From Comedy Calendar

item.title: Wingman Wednesdays Miami’s Social Comedy Show
item.description:
Wednesday, July 1, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: Blackbird Ordinary, Southwest 1st Avenue 729, Miami, FL, 33130, United States

Blackbird Ordinary. Meet new people. Laugh at hilarious dating stories. Join the conversation. Ladies drink free during showtime 9-10:30 PM. A fun night out where people happen to meet. Wingman Wednesdays is Miami’s social comedy show where dating, relationships, and modern romance become the night’s entertainment. Hosted by Miami’s Best Comedian Manny Garavito and Miami’s Best Master of Ceremonies Luke Prince, the show stirs up live comedy, tasteful crowd interaction, audience stories, terrible dating advice, red flags, green flags, first dates on the spot, and the hilarious realities of navigating Miami’s social scene. Whether you’re single, taken, in the alphabet, somewhere in between, or just here to watch the tea spill, you’ll be part of a night filled with laughs, unexpected moments, and real conversations. Audience participation is encouraged but never required, making it just as fun to watch as it is to jump in. Bring your friends, your situationships, your worst dating stories, but most importantly your sense of humor. Ladies drink free during showtime from 9:00 PM to 10:30 PM. Comedy. Cocktails. Questionable decisions. Topics include but are not limited to: Brickell finance bros Yacht season Sugar daddy retirement plans Instagram followers “What do you do?” stress tests Penthouse with air matresses Dating apps Gym culture Plastic surgery Spiritual girls How to Ghost 101 Toxic Green Flags …And enough unsolicited dating advice to keep everyone single a little longer. Come watch. Come participate. Or just sit back and enjoy the turbulence. Ticket Options General Admission (FREE: frist come, first serve)First come, first served. Whatever seats are not purchased will be available to anyone who arrives early. Once they’re gone, they’re gone. $25 Guaranteed Seating + Wingmen for HireSkip the rush and secure your seats in advance. Plus, you’ll be guaranteed an opportunity to join Manny and Luke on stage for live crowd interaction, dating advice, matchmaking attempts, relationship advice, or whatever romantic turbulence the evening encounters. Limited spots available. $50 VIP Flight Crew Experience (Guaranteed Seating, Wingmen for Hire, VIP Bottle Afterparty Access AND Merch)Everything included: guaranteed seating, Wingman for hire, access to our bottle section for the afterparty with Manny and Luke, plus exclusive Miami Comedy merch to take home. VERY Limited spots.

From Comedy Calendar

item.title: "Còn nước, còn tát": The Ebbs and Flows of Water across Vietnamese Anglophone Cultural Production
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 6 PM

Location: Herring Hall

  Speaker: Karen Siu Doctoral Candidate Thesis Defense Department: English Location: Herring Hall 255 As a child and grandchild of Vietnamese refugees, my scholarship is personal and political, purposely following feminist and Ethnic Studies methodologies. It intertwines oral histories, creative nonfiction, and art with academic writing. This project's hybrid form is meant to be an innovation and contribution to the field of Vietnamese American Studies, which has often separated the genres of creative and academic writing rather than intertwining them. I ask, why water remains such an enduring symbol for Vietnamese diasporic writers, scholars, and artists, when it is often a painful reminder of war. When the capital of South Việt Nam, Sài Gòn, fell to the North Vietnamese Army, effectively ending the Việt Nam War on April 30, 1975, the date became known as ngày mất nước, the day we lost the country, we lost the water. In Vietnamese, nước (pronounced nyúk or \nü-äk\) means water, homeland, country, or nation. Fifty years after the war, it is clear that war and colonialism devastated the environment of Việt Nam, but what does it mean when the identity of the land, the water, and its people is also devastated? What does it mean to lose the water? This project pursues these questions at this poignant time. I examine the beginnings of nước in Vietnamese folklore alongside Vietnamese Anglophone Cultural Production, including literature, art, and war memorials and memorializations, in order to trace the transformation of nước before and after the Việt Nam War. I claim that Vietnamese Anglophone cultural works share an intimacy with water, deviating from a nước associated with the Vietnam War and towards a nước that instead emphasizes how our community survives and resists. I put forward nước as a cultural framework that is feminist, antiwar, and decolonial. Ultimately, I argue that these archives offer an ecofeminist, transcorporeal vision of hope and resilience in the wake of war, imperialism, and colonialism that gestures back to and expands on precolonial conceptions of nước. By ecofeminist and transcorporeal, I aim to show the fluidity and interconnectedness between gendered, reproductive bodies and water challenges the tendency to dichotomize humans and nature.   https://riceuniversity.zoom.us/j/99222333231?pwd=4PxBl3d0j2kfAz4vioBaDGTqZ23w1p.1 (Department : English)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

item.title: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas Showcase (Early)
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino, 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV, 89103, United States

This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Official Tickets Resale Tickets Comedy Cellar Las Vegas brings one of the most legendary names in stand-up comedy to the heart of Las Vegas. Located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino, this intimate comedy club is the West Coast home of New York City’s world-famous Comedy Cellar, the venue that helped launch the careers of comedy icons including Dave Chappelle, Chris Rock, Jon Stewart, Ray Romano, Amy Schumer, Aziz Ansari, and countless others. Unlike traditional comedy shows featuring a single headliner, Comedy Cellar presents a carefully curated lineup of professional comedians every night. Audiences can expect multiple performers during each show, featuring a mix of nationally touring headliners, television favorites, Comedy Central veterans, Netflix performers, and rising stars from around the world. No two shows are exactly alike, making every visit a unique experience. Surprise drop-in appearances from major comedians performing elsewhere in Las Vegas have become part of the club’s reputation, giving audiences the chance to see comedy superstars in an intimate setting. Event Details Venue: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino Location: 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89103 Show Schedule: Nightly performances throughout the year Age Requirement: Recommended for audiences 18+ due to mature comedy content About the Venue Opened in 2018, Comedy Cellar Las Vegas was designed to recreate the atmosphere and energy of the original Greenwich Village club that has become one of the most respected comedy venues in the world. The 300-seat showroom offers excellent sightlines and an intimate setting that allows audiences to experience stand-up comedy exactly as it was meant to be seen: up close and personal. The venue combines the excitement of a Las Vegas night out with the authentic feel of a classic New York comedy club. Guests can enjoy a comfortable showroom environment while watching some of the best comedians working today perform in a setting that encourages spontaneity, crowd interaction, and unforgettable moments. Conveniently located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino just minutes from the Las Vegas Strip, the venue offers easy access for both tourists and local comedy fans. What to Expect Expect a fast-paced evening featuring multiple comedians performing throughout the show. The Comedy Cellar is known for showcasing a wide variety of comedic styles, from observational humor and storytelling to sharp social commentary, crowd work, and cutting-edge stand-up. Whether you’re discovering a future comedy star or watching a seasoned veteran perfect their craft, every show delivers the authentic comedy club experience that has made the Comedy Cellar one of the most respected brands in stand-up comedy. For comedy fans visiting Las Vegas, Comedy Cellar is widely considered one of the best places in the city to experience live stand-up comedy any night of the week.

From Comedy Calendar

item.title: Wingman THURSDAY Miami’s Social Comedy Show
item.description:
Thursday, July 2, 2026 at 9 PM

Location: Blackbird Ordinary, Southwest 1st Avenue 729, Miami, FL, 33130, United States

One night only on a Thursday! Meet new people, laugh with friends, enjoy cocktails, and experience Miami's most social comedy show. NOTE: This is a one-time THURSDAY event this week. This show is weekly on Wednesdays! A fun night out where people happen to meet. Wingman THURSDAY is Miami’s social comedy show where dating, relationships, and modern romance become the night’s entertainment. Hosted by Miami’s Best Comedian Manny Garavito and Miami’s Best Master of Ceremonies Luke Prince, the show stirs up live comedy, tasteful crowd interaction, audience stories, terrible dating advice, red flags, green flags, first dates on the spot, and the hilarious realities of navigating Miami’s social scene. Whether you’re single, taken, in the alphabet, somewhere in between, or just here to watch the tea spill, you’ll be part of a night filled with laughs, unexpected moments, and real conversations. Audience participation is encouraged but never required, making it just as fun to watch as it is to jump in. Bring your friends, your situationships, your worst dating stories, but most importantly your sense of humor. Ladies drink free during the beginning of showtime from 9 PM to 10 PM. Comedy. Cocktails. Questionable decisions. Topics include but are not limited to: Brickell finance bros Yacht season Sugar daddy retirement plans Instagram followers “What do you do?” stress tests Penthouse with air matresses Dating apps Gym culture Plastic surgery Spiritual girls How to Ghost 101 Toxic Green Flags …And enough unsolicited dating advice to keep everyone single a little longer. Come watch. Come participate. Or just sit back and enjoy the turbulence. Ticket Options General Admission (FREE: first come, first serve)First come, first served. Whatever seats are not purchased will be available to anyone who arrives early. Once they’re gone, they’re gone. $25 Guaranteed Seating + Wingmen for HireSkip the rush and secure your seats in advance. Plus, you’ll be guaranteed an opportunity to join Manny and Luke on stage for live crowd interaction, dating advice, matchmaking attempts, relationship advice, or whatever romantic turbulence the evening encounters. Limited spots available. $50 VIP Flight Crew Experience (Guaranteed Seating, Wingmen for Hire, VIP Bottle Afterparty Access AND Merch)Everything included: guaranteed seating, Wingman for hire, access to our bottle section for the afterparty with Manny and Luke, plus exclusive Miami Comedy merch to take home. VERY Limited spots.

Event Details Venue: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino Location: 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89103 Show Schedule: Nightly performances throughout the year Age Requirement: Recommended for audiences 18+ due to mature comedy content About the Venue Opened in 2018, Comedy Cellar Las Vegas was designed to recreate the atmosphere and energy of the original Greenwich Village club that has become one of the most respected comedy venues in the world. The 300-seat showroom offers excellent sightlines and an intimate setting that allows audiences to experience stand-up comedy exactly as it was meant to be seen: up close and personal. The venue combines the excitement of a Las Vegas night out with the authentic feel of a classic New York comedy club. Guests can enjoy a comfortable showroom environment while watching some of the best comedians working today perform in a setting that encourages spontaneity, crowd interaction, and unforgettable moments. Conveniently located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino just minutes from the Las Vegas Strip, the venue offers easy access for both tourists and local comedy fans. What to Expect Expect a fast-paced evening featuring multiple comedians performing throughout the show. The Comedy Cellar is known for showcasing a wide variety of comedic styles, from observational humor and storytelling to sharp social commentary, crowd work, and cutting-edge stand-up. Whether you’re discovering a future comedy star or watching a seasoned veteran perfect their craft, every show delivers the authentic comedy club experience that has made the Comedy Cellar one of the most respected brands in stand-up comedy. For comedy fans visiting Las Vegas, Comedy Cellar is widely considered one of the best places in the city to experience live stand-up comedy any night of the week.

item.title: Novel Computational Representations of TCRs and pMHCs Incorporating Structural Information for Translational Applications
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 2 PM

Location: Duncan Hall

  Speaker: Jared Slone Doctoral Candidate Thesis Defense Department: Computer Science Location: Duncan Hall 1049 The ability to accurately predict TCR-pHLA binding specificity would have major impacts on infectious disease research, autoimmunity, and personalized cancer immunotherapy. Experimental characterization of TCR-pHLA binding specificity is labor-intensive and costly, however, and, despite significant advances in machine learning, computationally predicting TCR binding specificity remains difficult. This difficulty arises from both the immense combinatorial space of possible TCR-pHLA pairings and the fact that the proteins in question possess high degrees of freedom, making it difficult to model their physical interactions. This thesis advances the computational characterization of TCR-pHLA interactions through the development of novel computational methods for modeling, representing, and predicting the behavior of TCR-pHLA pairs.   Central to this thesis is the use of three-dimensional structural information when predicting TCR-pHLA binding specificity. First, this thesis introduces STAG, a graph convolutional neural network for predicting TCR-pHLA binding specificity from modeled protein structures. STAG incorporates geometric and physicochemical information into a novel graph representation of TCR-pHLA complexes. The results from this work demonstrate that using holistic structural representations of the proteins as input outperforms prior structure-based approaches for binding prediction which were based on interprotein contacts. This thesis next presents STAG-LLM, a multimodal machine learning architecture that integrates protein language models with structure-based geometric learning. By combining amino acid sequence-derived embeddings with graph representations of three-dimensional protein structure, STAG-LLM achieves improved predictive performance and increased data efficiency compared to approaches that use sequence or structure alone. Recognizing that TCR-pHLA interactions are inherently dynamic, this thesis further introduces STEGG, a computational framework for generating structural ensembles of TCR-pHLA complexes. Through domain-specific conformational sampling and optimization techniques, STEGG efficiently explores the conformational landscape of TCR-pHLA interactions and generates diverse conformational ensembles without the computational expense of molecular dynamics simulations. Collectively, these contributions establish new computational paradigms for computationally representing TCR-pMHC structures, modeling their flexibility, and predicting their interactions. More broadly, this thesis demonstrates advances in structural bioinformatics that improve the computational characterization of TCR-pHLA interactions and aid in the development of personalized immunotherapies. (Department : Computer Science)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

item.title: Real and reciprocal space topology in rare earth intermetallics
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Brockman Hall for Physics

  Speaker: Kevin Allen Doctoral Candidate Thesis Defense Department: Physics & Astronomy Location: Brockman Hall for Physics 200 Topology in magnetic materials manifests across both real and reciprocal space, encompassing exotic band crossings in momentum space and nontrivial spin textures in real space. In strongly correlated and rare earth systems, these two aspects of topology are intertwined through the interplay of magnetic order, spin-orbit coupling, and electronic structure, giving rise to emergent phenomena including large anomalous Hall responses, extremely large magnetoresistance, and field-tunable soliton excitations. This dissertation presents four projects unified by this theme, spanning magnetic topological semimetals, atomically-sharp spin textures, strongly correlated heavy fermions, and spin-polarized surface catalysis. The first project identifies EuGa4 as a magnetic Weyl nodal ring semimetal, in which mirror symmetry protection stabilizes closed nodal line rings near the Fermi level in the spin-polarized state. Using angle-resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES), quantum oscillation (QO) measurements, and density functional theory (DFT), we characterize the Weyl nodal ring states and their Landau quantization. The associated high carrier mobility gives rise to a transverse magnetoresistance ex- ceeding 200, 000% at 2 K and 14 T, more than two orders of magnitude large than any previously known magnetic topological semimetal, with non-saturating behavior up to 40 T explained by a theoretical model rooted in the nodal ring electronic structure. The second project demonstrates that the square-net rare earth compound EuRhAl4Si2 hosts atomically-sharp, field-tunable one-dimensional magnetic solitons stabilized by the competition between frustrated Ruderman-Kittel-Kasuya-Yosida (RKKY) exchange and uniaxial magnetic anisotropy. Magnetization measurements reveal a robust 1/3 magnetization plateau with fine steps corresponding to discrete soliton insertions, confirmed by anisotropic magnetotransport on a Focus ion beam (FIB)-fabricated microstructure. Neutron diffraction establishes the collinear ferrimagnetic ↑↑↓ ground state, while magnetic force microscopy (MFM) directly images the real space soliton lattice. First-principles exchange parameters and an effective J1–J2–K Heisenberg model, supplemented by Monte Carlo simulations, reproduce the observed field evolution and identify the soliton defects as the fundamental excitation. We further explore related square-net compounds by studying the series RTM4M'2 (R = Eu, Gd; T = Rh, Ir; M = Al, Ga; M' = Si, Ge), to identify additional candidates for hosting magnetic solitons and examine how structural and chemical tuning controls their stability. The third project reports a large anomalous Hall conductivity of approximately 1.6×104 Ω−1cm−1 in the centrosymmetric Kondo lattice compound Ce3Al11, a moderate heavy fermion with Kondo temperature TK = 10 K and a Sommerfeld coefficient of γ ≈ 100 mJ/molK2. The anomalous Hall effect (AHE) is stricly confined to the incommensurate antiferromagnetic phase below TN , and its temperature dependence reveals a crossover from extrinsic skew scattering near TN to an intrinsic Berry curvature dominated response at low temperature, consistent with the progressive onset of Kondo coherence. We attribute the symmetry breaking necessary to generate finite Berry curvature to the combination of incommensurate magnetic order and a Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction at the noncentrosymmetric CeII site establishing a route to large anomalous Hall responses in heavy fermion antiferromagnets without structural chirality or band inversion. The fourth project investigates how ferromagnetism in L10-CoPt enhances oxygen surface binding relevant to the oxygen reduction reaction. DFT calculations reveal than spin-polarized Co-d states, through exchange splitting of the minority spin channel toward the Fermi level, strengthen hybridization with O-p states and enhance adsorption energies by approximately 0.1 eV per oxygen atom relative to the non- magnetic case. By systematically varying the Pt overlayer thickness, we demonstrate that Co proximity to the surface amplifies these magnetic exchange effects, while the antiparallel oxygen spin configuration consistently yields the strongest binding. These results refine the d-band center model to incorporate spin-dependent hybridization and suggest pathways for optimizing catalytic performance through control of magnetic and structural degrees of freedom.https://riceuniversity.zoom.us/j/8841232928?pwd=Rys1Vm1TQ0RSR2twT1RiQkNzRDN2QT09 (https://riceuniversity.zoom.us/j/8841232928?pwd=Rys1Vm1TQ0RSR2twT1RiQkNzRDN2QT09) (Department : Physics & Astronomy)

item.title: Timmy No Brakes LIVE at Pittsburgh Improv
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Pittsburgh Improv, 166 E. Bridge St, Homestead, Pennsylvania, 15120, United States

Official TicketsResale Tickets About the Performer Timmy No Brakes is comedy’s most unhinged rising star. A Kill Tony viral menace and Golden Ticket winner, Timmy has exploded onto the national comedy scene with his off-the-rails energy and deranged confidence that has audiences losing it from the first moment he hits the stage. Known for his high-octane rants, heterosexual character work, and pure uncut stupidity delivered with absolute conviction, Timmy No Brakes is the kind of comedian you have to see live to truly believe. His Kill Tony appearances have racked up millions of views online, turning him into one of the most talked-about new voices in stand-up comedy. If you’re looking for a comedy show that’s completely unpredictable, wildly funny, and unlike anything you’ve seen before, Timmy No Brakes is your guy. Buckle up — this show is going to be a ride. Event Details Date: Friday, July 3, 2026 Time: 7:00 PM (Doors open at 5:30 PM) Venue: Pittsburgh Improv, 166 E. Bridge St, Homestead, PA 15120 Age Restriction: Ages 21 and up Additional Shows: A late show is also available at 9:30 PM on Friday, July 3, and two shows on Saturday, July 4 at 6:30 PM and 9:00 PM. About the Venue The Pittsburgh Improv is located at 166 E. Bridge Street in Homestead, PA — just minutes from downtown Pittsburgh. As part of the nationally recognized Improv Comedy Club chain, the Pittsburgh Improv is one of the premier comedy venues in the region. The club features a full-service restaurant and bar, making it a perfect destination for a complete night out. The intimate showroom seats approximately 300 guests, ensuring every seat has a great sightline to the stage. The venue’s lively atmosphere is ideal for the kind of high-energy comedy that Timmy No Brakes delivers. Parking is available nearby, and the venue is easily accessible from major highways. Note that this show falls on the July 4th holiday weekend, so plan ahead for traffic and parking. The Pittsburgh Improv is known for bringing top national touring comedians to the Pittsburgh area. What to Expect Timmy No Brakes delivers a stand-up performance that is completely unhinged, wildly energetic, and absolutely hilarious. This is a solo stand-up show — expect 60–75 minutes of non-stop, unpredictable comedy that will have you in tears. His shows are known for being spontaneous and electric. Timmy’s comedy blends absurdist character work with rapid-fire observations and crowd interaction. No two shows are the same, and Pittsburgh audiences are in for a treat on the July 4th holiday weekend. Please note: This is an ages 21 and up event. Valid ID is required at the door. item.description:

Official TicketsResale Tickets About the Performer Beth Stelling is one of the most celebrated stand-up comedians working today, known for her razor-sharp wit, deeply personal storytelling, and fearless honesty on stage. Her comedy blends confessional vulnerability with biting humor, tackling topics like relationships, identity, and the absurdities of everyday life with a refreshing authenticity. Stelling has appeared on Netflix, Comedy Central, and Late Night with Seth Meyers, and her acclaimed special Girl Daddy earned widespread critical praise for its emotional depth and comedic precision. Event Details Date: Friday, July 3, 2026 Show Time: 7:30 PM (doors open earlier) Venue: Jimmy Kimmel’s Comedy Club, 3545 S Las Vegas Blvd, Las Vegas, NV 89109 Age Restriction: 18+ Event Ticket Prices: From $63 About the Venue Jimmy Kimmel’s Comedy Club is one of the premier comedy destinations on the Las Vegas Strip, located on the iconic LINQ Promenade at 3545 S Las Vegas Blvd. The intimate venue seats approximately 300 guests and features a state-of-the-art sound system, excellent sightlines from every seat, and a full bar service throughout the show. The club’s cozy atmosphere creates an up-close-and-personal comedy experience unlike the large arena shows Vegas is known for. Parking is available at the LINQ Hotel parking garage, and the venue is easily accessible via the Las Vegas Monorail. The LINQ Promenade itself offers a variety of dining and entertainment options before and after the show, making it a perfect evening destination. What to Expect Beth Stelling delivers a headline solo stand-up performance packed with her signature blend of personal storytelling and sharp observational comedy. Expect an intimate, high-energy show where Stelling connects directly with the audience through her candid, relatable humor. Her sets are known for building emotional momentum — drawing laughs while also leaving audiences genuinely moved. This is a rare opportunity to see one of comedy’s most talked-about voices in an intimate Las Vegas club setting. Arrive early to secure the best seats and enjoy a drink before the show. Ticket Information Tickets start from $63 and are available through official and resale channels. Official TicketsResale Tickets This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you.

Every purchase supports the Miami Comedy community, helping us grow and continue spreading laughter across the city. This collectible shirt is one-of-a-kind—once it’s sold out, it’s gone forever. Don’t miss your chance to own a stylish piece of Miami comedy history and be part of something truly special. Grab yours now and celebrate 10 years of laughs with us! Sizes S – XL available. Thank You Miami Beer House and Cocina is the newest hip bar located in Flagler and 17th. This bar gives thanks to the city by offering a large selection of beers and food as well as LIVE entertainment. About Miami Comedy MiamiComedy.com has produced independent comedy events for years. Over time we’ve built a platform where you can laugh everyday in Miami! We present to you authentic local and national performers and curate entertaining live shows for locals who love to laugh and enjoy the true art of stand up comedy. As seen on Amazon Prime Video “Respect the Light” the Miami New Times, Culture Crusaders, the Miami Improv and UNATION. We make each experience unique with its own environment, line up, drinking, social groups and dining menus! Questions? Special Occasions? Event Planning? Birthdays? item.title: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas (Late)
item.description:
Friday, July 3, 2026 at 9:30 PM

Location: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino, 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV, 89103, United States

This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Official Tickets Resale Tickets Comedy Cellar Las Vegas brings one of the most legendary names in stand-up comedy to the heart of Las Vegas. Located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino, this intimate comedy club is the West Coast home of New York City’s world-famous Comedy Cellar, the venue that helped launch the careers of comedy icons including Dave Chappelle, Chris Rock, Jon Stewart, Ray Romano, Amy Schumer, Aziz Ansari, and countless others. Unlike traditional comedy shows featuring a single headliner, Comedy Cellar presents a carefully curated lineup of professional comedians every night. Audiences can expect multiple performers during each show, featuring a mix of nationally touring headliners, television favorites, Comedy Central veterans, Netflix performers, and rising stars from around the world. No two shows are exactly alike, making every visit a unique experience. Surprise drop-in appearances from major comedians performing elsewhere in Las Vegas have become part of the club’s reputation, giving audiences the chance to see comedy superstars in an intimate setting. Event Details Venue: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino Location: 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89103 Show Schedule: Nightly performances throughout the year Age Requirement: Recommended for audiences 18+ due to mature comedy content About the Venue Opened in 2018, Comedy Cellar Las Vegas was designed to recreate the atmosphere and energy of the original Greenwich Village club that has become one of the most respected comedy venues in the world. The 300-seat showroom offers excellent sightlines and an intimate setting that allows audiences to experience stand-up comedy exactly as it was meant to be seen: up close and personal. The venue combines the excitement of a Las Vegas night out with the authentic feel of a classic New York comedy club. Guests can enjoy a comfortable showroom environment while watching some of the best comedians working today perform in a setting that encourages spontaneity, crowd interaction, and unforgettable moments. Conveniently located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino just minutes from the Las Vegas Strip, the venue offers easy access for both tourists and local comedy fans. What to Expect Expect a fast-paced evening featuring multiple comedians performing throughout the show. The Comedy Cellar is known for showcasing a wide variety of comedic styles, from observational humor and storytelling to sharp social commentary, crowd work, and cutting-edge stand-up. Whether you’re discovering a future comedy star or watching a seasoned veteran perfect their craft, every show delivers the authentic comedy club experience that has made the Comedy Cellar one of the most respected brands in stand-up comedy. For comedy fans visiting Las Vegas, Comedy Cellar is widely considered one of the best places in the city to experience live stand-up comedy any night of the week.

item.title: Freedom Over Texas 2026
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 3 PM

Location: Eleanor Tinsley Park, 18-3600 Allen Pkwy, Houston, TX, 77019, United States

Friday, July 4, 3 pm to 10 pmEleanor Tinsley Park on Buffalo Bayou The most anticipated summer fun in the City of Houston, celebrating its 39th year, the annual Freedom Over Texas returns on Saturday, July 4 at Eleanor Tinsley and Sam Houston Parks. From 3 pm – 10 pm, this extraordinary, fun-filled celebration highlights the traditions of Independence Day, as well as honoring the nation’s 250-year anniversary. As an added bonus Houston Food Bank will receive a percentage of concessions as a donation (up to $10,000), helping to fill an extraordinary demand in our regional community. The Freedom Stage will be showcasing Los Lonely Boys to kick off the night, followed by Collective Soul opening for our headliner Keith Urban, who is sure to entertain music fans through the evening. For friends, families, and fans in town for soccer, there is something for everyone to enjoy, including Fútbol Park, Liberty Park, the Kids Zone, Budweiser Zone, and Dr. Pepper’s Flavor Zone. All of it leads up to a spectacularly choreographed, Texas-sized fireworks finale. Freedom Over Texas draws an average crowd of 35,000 attendees each year and is broadcast live on ABC KTRK Channel 13 across Houston, with outreach to television affiliates statewide to simulcast portions of the event. Learn More and Purchase Tickets

From Houston Food Bank

item.title: Benji Brown LIVE at Arlington Improv (4th of July Show)
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Arlington Improv, 309 Curtis Mathes Way #147, Arlington, TX, 76018, United States Of America

Official TicketsResale Tickets This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. About the Performer Benji Brown is an urban comedian setting a new standard in comedy. He toured with Martin Lawrence’s 2018 LIT AF Tour in major arenas across the country and is now the host of Martin Lawrence’s “Yall Know What It Is Tour.” He headlined his own “Act Crazy If You Want To” national tour in 2019, making him one of the only urban comedians — alongside Kevin Hart — with the clout to pull off a solo national tour of that magnitude. His viral character KiKi has garnered over 3 million views, cementing him as one of the next major urban comedic household names. Event Details Date: Saturday, July 4, 2026 (Independence Day) Time: 7:00 PM (Doors open 6:00 PM) Venue: Arlington Improv, 309 Curtis Mathes Way #147, Arlington, TX 76018 Age Restriction: 18+ Price Range: $20 to $60 per person About the Venue Arlington Improv is located in the Highlands Shopping Center in Arlington, TX, across from Torchy’s Tacos. It is an intimate comedy club featuring a full bar and restaurant where guests enjoy world-renowned and local comics in a lively, up-close setting. The venue also hosts Black Dog Pizza Arcade, a full-scale free-play retro arcade featuring more than 30 local craft beers. Seating is assigned based on date of purchase. A 2-item food or beverage minimum per person applies once seated, and there is an 18% service charge on all checks in the showroom. What to Expect Celebrate the 4th of July with a night of non-stop laughs as Benji Brown headlines Arlington Improv. Expect his signature urban humor, viral character work, and the high-energy delivery that has made him one of the most exciting comedians on the national touring circuit. This is an 18+ event. Doors open at 6:00 PM. Food and beverage service ends approximately 45 minutes into the show. All sales are final, no refunds or exchanges. Ticket Information Tickets range from $20 to $60 per person. Purchase official tickets or find resale options below. Official TicketsResale Tickets

From Comedy Calendar

item.title: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas Showcase (Early)
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 7 PM

Location: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino, 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV, 89103, United States

This post contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission at no extra cost to you. Official Tickets Resale Tickets Comedy Cellar Las Vegas brings one of the most legendary names in stand-up comedy to the heart of Las Vegas. Located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino, this intimate comedy club is the West Coast home of New York City’s world-famous Comedy Cellar, the venue that helped launch the careers of comedy icons including Dave Chappelle, Chris Rock, Jon Stewart, Ray Romano, Amy Schumer, Aziz Ansari, and countless others. Unlike traditional comedy shows featuring a single headliner, Comedy Cellar presents a carefully curated lineup of professional comedians every night. Audiences can expect multiple performers during each show, featuring a mix of nationally touring headliners, television favorites, Comedy Central veterans, Netflix performers, and rising stars from around the world. No two shows are exactly alike, making every visit a unique experience. Surprise drop-in appearances from major comedians performing elsewhere in Las Vegas have become part of the club’s reputation, giving audiences the chance to see comedy superstars in an intimate setting. Event Details Venue: Comedy Cellar Las Vegas at Rio Hotel & Casino Location: 3700 W Flamingo Rd, Las Vegas, NV 89103 Show Schedule: Nightly performances throughout the year Age Requirement: Recommended for audiences 18+ due to mature comedy content About the Venue Opened in 2018, Comedy Cellar Las Vegas was designed to recreate the atmosphere and energy of the original Greenwich Village club that has become one of the most respected comedy venues in the world. The 300-seat showroom offers excellent sightlines and an intimate setting that allows audiences to experience stand-up comedy exactly as it was meant to be seen: up close and personal. The venue combines the excitement of a Las Vegas night out with the authentic feel of a classic New York comedy club. Guests can enjoy a comfortable showroom environment while watching some of the best comedians working today perform in a setting that encourages spontaneity, crowd interaction, and unforgettable moments. Conveniently located inside the Rio Hotel & Casino just minutes from the Las Vegas Strip, the venue offers easy access for both tourists and local comedy fans. What to Expect Expect a fast-paced evening featuring multiple comedians performing throughout the show. The Comedy Cellar is known for showcasing a wide variety of comedic styles, from observational humor and storytelling to sharp social commentary, crowd work, and cutting-edge stand-up. Whether you’re discovering a future comedy star or watching a seasoned veteran perfect their craft, every show delivers the authentic comedy club experience that has made the Comedy Cellar one of the most respected brands in stand-up comedy. For comedy fans visiting Las Vegas, Comedy Cellar is widely considered one of the best places in the city to experience live stand-up comedy any night of the week.

From Comedy Calendar

Known for his hilarious parody songs and outrageous live performances, Weird Al delivers a spectacular concert experience packed with comedy, music, and non-stop entertainment. Event Details Date: Saturday, July 4, 2026 Time: 8:00 PM (Doors open 7:00 PM) Venue: The Colosseum at Caesars Windsor Address: 377 Riverside Drive East, Windsor, Ontario, Canada N9A 7H7 Age Requirement: All Ages Welcome Parking: Complimentary parking in attached six-level parking garage About “Weird Al” Yankovic What to Expect A high-energy, full-production concert featuring Weird Al’s greatest parody hits Multiple costume changes and theatrical set pieces throughout the show Classic parodies including “Eat It,” “Amish Paradise,” “White & Nerdy,” and more Special guest Puddles Pity Party joining the tour An all-ages family-friendly comedy concert experience Unforgettable moments of laughter and musical brilliance Venue Information The Colosseum at Caesars Windsor is one of Canada’s premier entertainment venues, located at 377 Riverside Drive East in Windsor, Ontario, just across the border from Detroit. The venue features world-class amenities, complimentary parking, and easy access from both Canada and the United States. Ticket Information Tickets for “Weird Al” Yankovic: Bigger & Weirder 2026 Tour start from $118. There is an overall 4-ticket limit per order. item.title: Red, White & Bloom
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: McGovern Centennial Gardens, 1500 Hermann Drive, Houston, TX, 77004, United States

Celebrate the Fourth of July surrounded by beautiful blooms, festive fun, and one of Houston’s most beloved fireworks displays! Join us for “Red, White & Bloom” on Saturday, July 4, from 8 – 11 p.m. in the McGovern Centennial Gardens for a fantastic summer evening. This year is extra special as we mark America’s 250th birthday with a celebration packed with birthday party vibes! Enjoy free treats, family-friendly activities, music, lawn games, and more before the night wraps up with a spectacular view of the fireworks show from Miller Outdoor Theatre. Best of all, Red, White & Bloom is FREE and open to the public! Bring a blanket, gather your friends and family, and stroll through the vibrant garden spaces before settling in for the show. Spend Independence Day in this iconic spot, under the stars—a perfect setting for summer memories! The fireworks show at Miller Outdoor Theatre is set to start at approximately 9:45 p.m. The complimentary food items are while supplies last. Additional food vendors will be offering items for purchase by Miller Outdoor Theatre.

From Hermann Park Conservancy

Event details

item.title: Red, White & Cruise (SOLD OUT!)
item.description:
Saturday, July 4, 2026 at 8:30 PM

Location: Lake Plaza

THIS EVENT HAS SOLD OUT! IF YOU WOULD LIKE TO BE PLACED ON A WAITLIST IF ANY SPOTS OPEN UP, PLEASE EMAIL INFO@HERMANNPARK.ORG. Join us for Red, White & Cruise where you can watch Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show light up the sky from the peaceful waters of Hermann Park, with a special pedal boat rental from 8:30–10:30 p.m. on Saturday, July 4! As we celebrate 250 years of American independence, gather your crew and watch the sky come alive with fireworks while cruising across the lake in your own pedal boat under the stars. Saturday, July 4 8:30 – 10:30 p.m.  🎟️ $150 per boat (up to 4 people) includes: 🕘 Boat rental up to 2 hours 🍿 Drinks & snacks 🎊 Festive accessories 🅿️ Reserved parking in Lot I near Lake Plaza ⛵ Board anytime between 8:30–9:30 p.m. and enjoy the show from the water (fireworks begin at approximately 9:45 p.m., boats must return once fireworks finish) BOOK YOUR BOAT HERE!  🚨 Important Info: One rider per boat must be 18+ All riders 18+ must sign a digital waiver before boarding At least one rider must be 18+, with a physical government ID, and sign a waiver before boarding Tickets are non-refundable Event is rain or shine per Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan In the event of lightning, pedal boats will close, and refunds will be issued  The purchase of a ticket includes an up-to 2-hour pedal boat ride for up to 4 people to enjoy the sight of Miller Outdoor Theatre’s fireworks show, drinks, snacks, festive treats, and a reserved parking spot in Lot I near Lott Hall by Lake Plaza (starting at 8 p.m.). Only one ticket purchase of $150 is needed for a 4-person boat (i.e. it is the cost per boat, not per rider).  A reminder that at least one rider per boat must be 18 years or older.  All riders 18 years or older must sign a digital waiver before boarding. Tickets are non-refundable. We are following Miller Outdoor Theatre’s plan for their event and fireworks show which is rain or shine. If there is lightning in the area, we will close the pedal boats and ticket holders will receive a refund.  Please arrive early to allow time to park in Lot I as roads in the Park will be closed by HPD and closures are subject to change based on their purview.  

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Pitch in at Hermann Park
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 8:30 AM

Location: Lake Plaza

Give the Park you love a little extra care, and help us get ready for the world! Join us for “Pitch in at Hermann Park,” a hands-on volunteer series where community members of all ages come together to help care for Houston’s most cherished green spaces. We’re proud to work alongside FWC26Houston and Harris County–Houston Sports Authority in the Impact Houston 26 efforts to elevate the local environmental action on a global stage and build a lasting, positive legacy for our city. You’ll work alongside Conservancy staff on simple but meaningful projects like mulching, planting, and litter cleanup — the kind of small efforts that make a big impact for visitors, wildlife, and the health of the Park. This volunteer session will be in the Heart of the Park (6104 Hermann Park Drive, Houston Texas 77030). Volunteers will meet at the Pedal Boat Kiosk. Enter through Lot G (near the Zoo) or Lot E and park in Lot F.  Walk up the concrete sidewalk to the Pedal Boat Kiosk, and we will be watching for your group. No experience is needed — just comfortable clothes, closed-toe shoes, and a willingness to help. We’ll provide the tools, guidance, and a great way to spend your morning outdoors while meeting new people and giving back to the community. Whether you come with friends, family, coworkers, or on your own, your time truly matters here. Help us welcome the world to Hermann Park! SIGN UP HERE. Weather:   Volunteer projects are rain or shine, unless there is severe weather. Cancellations due to severe weather will be sent via text/email at least 1 hour before your shift. Use your best judgment when traveling in inclement weather. Questions? Email volunteer@hermannpark.org or call 713-524-5876. Remember, we are often out in the field, so email is the preferred method of getting in touch!

From Hermann Park Conservancy

item.title: Frame of Reference in UAV Search and Rescue: Exocentric vs. Egocentric View for Navigation, Target Detection, and Collision Awareness
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 4 PM

  Speaker: Yining Zhang Masters Candidate Thesis Defense Department: Psychology Unmanned Aerial Systems (UASs) have enabled search-and-rescue (SAR) missions to be safer, more efficient, and more effective due to their advanced mobility and sensing technologies. Traditionally, UASs employ a first-person, egocentric frame of reference (FOR), enabled by the camera system installed on the vehicle. However, this FOR provides limited visibility of the vehicle's surrounding environment, leading to blind spots. In contrast, research has shown that an exocentric, behind-the-vehicle FOR can lead to better mission outcomes and safer teleoperation. The current study compared operator performance and eye-movement patterns using the egocentric and exocentric FORs in a simulated SAR mission. A total of 37 participants performed three tasks using the two FORs, including collision-awareness, route-deviation detection, and target-detection tasks. The results showed that the effectiveness of the FORs was dependent on the task specifications. For the collision-awareness and target-detection tasks, there was no significant difference in response time (RT) or hit rate (H). For the route-deviation detection task, participants exhibited a speed-accuracy trade-off: they had a faster RT but lower H in the exocentric condition compared to the egocentric condition. Additionally, we found that participants in the exocentric condition attributed more visual attention to the camera view than in the egocentric condition, potentially due to the high salience of the vehicle body against the surrounding environment. Overall, the current study suggested that future UASs should employ a dynamic visual display, allowing the operator to choose the FOR that is more appropriate for the mission. https://riceuniversity.zoom.us/j/94483800467?pwd=XgoaQZA7ioB8HHGavYPyGnaDag6iDl.1 (Department : Psychology)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

item.title: K-Nexus: Hierarchical Gene-to-Cell AI Modeling for Perturbation-Induced Transcriptomic Shift Recovery
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 5 PM

  Speaker: Zihan Zhao Masters Candidate Thesis Defense Department: Electrical and Computer Engineering Predicting cellular responses to genetic and chemical perturbations can accelerate therapeutic discovery by reducing experimental screening. However, predicting post-perturbation gene expression, particularly for unseen perturbations, remains challenging because response signals are sparse, cellular responses are heterogeneous and often unpaired, and responsive genes are obscured by dominant non-responsive genes. We propose K-Nexus, a hierarchical gene-to-cell framework that formulates perturbation response prediction as distribution-shift recovery over responsive genes. K-Nexus encodes perturbation-conditioned regulatory interactions at the gene level and aggregates them into cell-level representations that preserve response heterogeneity. Attention-based gene importance, response-aware weighting, and group-based distributional training focus learning on genes driving perturbation-induced changes. K-Nexus also incorporates perturbation and regulatory graphs to capture functional relationships between targeted genes and downstream pathways. Across three single-cell perturbation benchmarks, K-Nexus outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, particularly in responsive-gene distributional alignment, demonstrating improved recovery of expression shifts and generalization to unseen perturbations.   (Department : Electrical and Computer Engineering)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

Event details

item.title: O⁺ Transport from the Ionospheric Cusp to the Inner Magnetosphere: A Global MHD and Test Particle Study
item.description:
Wednesday, July 8, 2026 at 5 PM

Location: Herman Brown Hall

  Speaker: Yiwen Zhu Doctoral Candidate Thesis Defense Department: Physics & Astronomy Location: Herman Brown Hall HBH 254 During geomagnetic storms, ionospheric oxygen (O⁺) can become a significant constituent of Earth's ring current, the current system whose buildup drives the main-phase depression of the surface magnetic field. The polar cusp is a principal source, feeding O⁺ outflow into the magnetotail lobe, but how much of this oxygen is delivered inward to the inner magnetosphere, and how it is energized along the way, remain poorly quantified, in part because the transport spans scales that no single model resolves.   In this thesis, we follow ionospheric oxygen from the cusp to the ring current using a global magnetohydrodynamic simulation (GAMERA/MAGE) coupled one-way to a test-particle tracer (CHIMP). Because MAGE includes an inner-magnetosphere model, it produces and resolves the mesoscale bursty bulk flows (BBFs) of the plasma sheet that drive earthward transport. With it, we identify a "drop-down" channel that controls the delivery to the inner magnetosphere: the dipolarization field carried by these flows pulls cusp oxygen from the lobe down to the equatorial plasma sheet, and the ions that experience it form a more efficient ring-current injection channel than those that convect down the tail unaided. Within an adiabatic (guiding-center) framework, we decompose the energy gain along the transport into its Fermi, betatron, and E×B contributions and quantify each, and we characterize where the oxygen departs from adiabatic behavior. To put these results on a quantitative footing, we weight the test particles by an O⁺ outflow rate computed directly from the simulation's MHD variables. For a real storm, this lets us quantify the absolute amount of cusp oxygen delivered to the inner magnetosphere and follow its contribution to the ring current through the storm, showing that a portion of the cusp oxygen is accelerated to ring-current energies and adds to the ring-current population. As a separate diagnostic, we characterize the energy and pitch-angle phase-space distributions, f(K, α), of the cusp oxygen at several locations along the transport and at ring-current entry, mapping how the population is energized and reshaped as it moves inward. Finally, we step back to the cusp itself. Forecasting where the cusp lies from the upstream solar wind is operationally important, yet no data-driven model has provided the cusp position directly. We develop a machine-learning model, gradient-boosted decision trees trained on DMSP spacecraft cusp crossings, that predicts the cusp location from solar-wind and interplanetary-magnetic-field conditions, offering a fast alternative to a full simulation. Together, these results advance two distinct fronts. The simulation and test-particle study closes a missing link in the cusp-to-ring-current pathway: it resolves how cusp oxygen reaches the inner magnetosphere, decomposes how the ions are energized along the way, and quantifies their absolute contribution to the storm-time ring current. The cusp machine-learning model is a separate, data-driven product: a fast predictor of the cusp location from the upstream solar wind, suited to operational use where a full simulation is impractical. Both improve how the entry of ionospheric heavy ions into the inner magnetosphere can be modeled and anticipated. (Department : Physics & Astronomy)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

item.title: Chemical Pathways to Earth's Habitability: Constraints from the Origin, Distribution, and Delivery of Life-Essential Elements in the Early Solar System
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 10 AM

Location: Keith-Wiess Geological Laboratories

  Speaker: Debjeet Pathak Doctoral Candidate Thesis Defense Department: Earth, Environmental and Planetary Sciences Location: Keith-Wiess Geological Laboratories The emergence and long-term habitability of Earth depend on the acquisition and redistribution of life-essential elements (LEEs), including C, N, P, S, H, and O, during planetary formation. This thesis investigates the chemical pathways that governed the origin, evolution, and delivery of these elements from the earliest stages of Solar System history to the formation of Earth. By integrating high-pressure-temperature experiments, meteoritic observations, geochemical reconstructions, and planetary accretion models, this work constrains the behavior of LEEs during core formation, planetary differentiation, and planetary growth. New experimental constraints on the partitioning of nitrogen and phosphorus between solid and liquid metallic alloys (Chapter 2 and 3), together with nitrogen partitioning between cohenite and metallic liquid (Chapter 4), are used to reconstruct the inventories of life-essential elements in the parent bodies of iron meteorites—the earliest differentiated planetesimals in the Solar System. Comparison of these reconstructed inventories with those of chondrites reveals the spatial and temporal evolution of life-essential elements during the earliest stages of Solar System formation and suggests that inner Solar system planetesimals were the major contributors of phosphorus and nitrogen to the growing Earth. Complementary electron probe microanalysis (EPMA) measurements significantly expand the existing nitrogen dataset for iron meteorites, providing improved constraints on their parent-body nitrogen inventories (Chapter 5). Furthermore, reconstructed zinc, sulfur, and gallium inventories provide evidence that disequilibrium condensation played an important role during the formation of these early planetary bodies (Chapter 6). Finally, numerical models of a Mars-sized last giant impactor demonstrate that its metallic core, rather than its silicate mantle, could have been an important source of volatile and life-essential elements delivered to the silicate Earth (Chapter 7). Collectively, this thesis provides new geochemical constraints on the origin, redistribution, and delivery of life-essential elements during planetary formation, offering a comprehensive framework for understanding the chemical pathways that ultimately led to Earth's habitability.   (Department : Earth, Environmental and Planetary Sciences)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

item.title: First Fridays Presents Leslie Archibald
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 7:30 PM

Location: Inprint House, 1520 W Main St, Houston, TX, 77006, United States

Free and open to the public Doors @ 7 pm Pop-up poet @ 7:30 pm Featured poet @ 7:40 pm Open mic @ 8:15 pm Inprint is proud to serve as the host for the First Fridays Reading Series. The First Fridays Reading Series is the oldest poetry series in Houston and was organized by Robert Clark from 1975-2019. Today, First Fridays events are curated and hosted by local community members. The July featured reader will be Leslie Archibald, a Houston-based writer. When she is not writing, you may find her photographing Houston’s unique character or dabbling in watercolors. Her visual and literary works appear in anthologies, lit magazines, and Houston galleries. Her chapbook Poems for a Broken 𝙼̶𝚊̶𝚛̶𝚛̶𝚒̶𝚊̶𝚐̶𝚎̶ Man (Finishing Line Press) is now available. Learn more about Leslie at lesliearchibaldwriter.com or @leslie.archibald. The opening pop-up poet will be Margo Davis, author of the recent poetry collection Uncoupling (Lamar University Press, 2026). Her poems appear in local outstanding journals such as equinox and Synkroniciti, as well as The Arkansas Review, Deep South, and Mackinaw Journal. Forthcoming poems will appear in The MacGuffin, as well as a series for The Ekphrastic Review. Her photographs have appeared in Barcelona galleries and several journals, and are forthcoming in Flyway. Margo resides in Houston but still thinks of herself as a New Orleanian. For more information about the First Fridays Reading Series, click here. To join the First Fridays Facebook group, click here.

From Inprint Houston

Event details

item.title: Programmable control of small non-coding RNA expression in mammalian cells with synthetic RNA polymerase III transactivators.
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Keck Hall

  Speaker: Matthew Demelo Doctoral Candidate Thesis Defense Department: BioSciences-Biochemistry and Cell Biology Location: Keck Hall 102 Reliable control of gene expression via synthetic genetic circuits is essential to develop next-generation therapies, to facilitate efficient bioindustrial processes, and to enable advanced methods in basic cell biology. Synthetic control of small non-coding RNAs (sncRNAs) is of immense interest due to their important roles in development and disease in mammals, as well as their central role in technologies such as CRISPR/Cas gene editing and RNA interference. RNA polymerase III (Pol III) is the canonical polymerase for sncRNA expression, but few tools for regulated Pol III expression exist, requiring expression from RNA polymerase II (Pol II) promoters. While there has been extensive development of synthetic regulatory methods for expressing protein-coding RNAs and large non-coding RNAs via Pol II, these methods are often ineffective for robust expression of functional sncRNAs and often require post-transcriptional processing for sncRNAs to achieve functionality, impairing the feasibility of translating sncRNA technologies into medical and industrial applications. Here, I develop a toolkit capable of highly programmable and multiplexable expression of synthetic sncRNAs using Synthetic Pol III Transcription Factors (Syn3TFs), facilitating far more effective and robust expression of sncRNAs in mammals than previous methods. Additionally, I outline concise design rules for the construction of minimal Pol III promoters and complementary Syn3TFs, facilitating seamless construction of orthogonal Pol III circuits with customizable outputs. Finally, I demonstrate the utility of Syn3TFs in performing complex genetic logic and acting as efficient sensors for continuous molecular recording of cellular events. This platform represents a crucial first step to expanding the arsenal of circuit construction strategies for sncRNA expression and provides a strong foundation for continued development of Pol III-specific synthetic regulators.   (Department : BioSciences-Biochemistry and Cell Biology)

From Rice University - Open to the Public

Event details

item.title: Programmable control of small non-coding RNA expression in mammalian cells with synthetic RNA polymerase III transactivators.
item.description:
Friday, July 10, 2026 at 8 PM

Location: Keck Hall

  Speaker: Matt Demelo Doctoral Candidate Thesis Defense Department: BioSciences-Biochemistry and Cell Biology Location: Keck Hall 102 Reliable control of gene expression via synthetic genetic circuits is essential to develop next-generation therapies, to facilitate efficient bioindustrial processes, and to enable advanced methods in basic cell biology. Synthetic control of small non-coding RNAs (sncRNAs) is of immense interest due to their important roles in development and disease in mammals, as well as their central role in technologies such as CRISPR/Cas gene editing and RNA interference. RNA polymerase III (RNAP3) is the canonical polymerase for sncRNA expression, but few tools for regulated RNAP3 expression exist, so genetic circuits requiring regulated sncRNA expression must use RNA polymerase II (RNAP2) promoters. While there has been extensive development of synthetic regulatory methods for expressing protein-coding RNAs and large non-coding RNAs via RNAP2, these methods are often ineffective for robust expression of functional sncRNAs and often require post-transcriptional processing for sncRNAs to achieve functionality, impairing the feasibility of translating sncRNA technologies into medical and industrial applications. Here, we develop a toolkit capable of highly programmable and multiplexable expression of synthetic sncRNAs using Synthetic RNAP3 Transcription Factors (Syn3TFs), achieving greater than 10-fold improvement in efficiency compared to analogous RNAP2-mediated methods. Additionally, we outline concise design rules for the construction of minimal RNAP3 promoters and complementary Syn3TFs, facilitating seamless construction of orthogonal RNAP3 circuits with customizable outputs. Finally, we demonstrate the utility of Syn3TFs in performing complex genetic logic and acting as efficient sensors for continuous molecular recording of cellular events. This platform represents a crucial first step to expanding the arsenal of circuit construction strategies for sncRNA expression and provides a strong foundation for continued development of RNAP3-specific synthetic regulators.   (Department : BioSciences-Biochemistry and Cell Biology)

From Rice University - Open to the Public
